citizen_summary: This new law, the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, aims to protect your digital personal information. It outlines how companies and government bodies must handle your data, giving you more control over it. You now have clear rights to know what data is collected, give consent for its use, and request its correction or deletion.
key_changes: You must give clear consent for your digital personal data to be processed, and you can easily withdraw this consent at any time.
You have the right to request information about your personal data, ask for corrections or updates, and even demand its erasure when no longer needed.
Companies must provide you with a clear notice, in your preferred language, explaining what personal data they will process and for what purpose.
Data Fiduciaries (organizations processing your data) are fully responsible for its protection and must promptly inform you and the new Data Protection Board about any data breaches.
You gain access to a readily available grievance redressal mechanism with data handlers and a Data Protection Board to address complaints about data breaches or rights violations.
affected_groups: Businesses and organizations (Data Fiduciaries) collecting and processing digital personal data in India or related to offering goods/services in India
Nominees for data rights (in case of Data Principal's death or incapacity)
rights_impact: This Act significantly strengthens the fundamental right to privacy by empowering individuals with greater control over their digital personal data, while also providing a framework for its lawful and responsible processing.
implementation_date: To be notified by the Central Government; different dates may apply for different provisions.
